metadata.title: Q US Badges for Deals and Pricing Codes

space.name: Digital UX Guidelines and Best Practices

content.value:
[For Qi Market Badging](https://qurate.atlassian.net/wiki/x/AQDdIg)

**Summary:** Overview of the Purpose, Design and Use of Badges for Promotions and Pricing Offers.

**Table of contents:**

16falsenonelisttrue

**To find the most up-to-date design specs and components** we use for our QVC pages, reference the [LIVE Design System in Figma](https://www.figma.com/design/7ATYi3dUVI9jokSSNR84EA/LIVE-Design-System?node-id=39-180&m=dev&focus-id=51-298)

---

### Overview

Badges serve as visual markers that help customers easily identify products associated with specific deals, pricing codes, and events. The aim is to enable quick recognition and streamline the shopping experience.

| **Old Image Badges** | **New HTML Badges** |
| --- | --- |
| badge_tsv.png | image2024-9-9_10-42-16.png?version=1&modificationDate=1725892937586&cacheVersion=1&api=v2 |

---

### UX Best Practices

#### Baymard Institute: Guideline #447

During testing, the Baymard Institute identified two crucial details for optimizing the use of labels and badges to highlight product differences:

1. **Use labels and badges sparingly**.
2. **Ensure labels and badges are meaningful and easy to see and understand**.

**Key Insights:**

* **Avoid Overuse:** If most items in product lists or search results have the same labels, the purchasing attribute is not unique. This can slow down the scanning process rather than aid it. Essentially, when everything is vying for attention, nothing stands out. The goal is to highlight products with unique features or those tailored to specific user needs — the ones actively sought after by particular customers.
* **Clarity of Labels:** If users don’t grasp what a label indicating a unique purchasing attribute means, they won’t confidently include or exclude products based on those labels.

#### Do:

1. **Keep Badge Promotions Clear and Concise:** Avoid clutter by ensuring promotions are straightforward.
2. **Provide Detailed Explanations in Badge Popups:** Ensure users can access comprehensive information easily.
3. **Use Consistent Badge Designs:** Maintain design consistency across product tiles to avoid confusion.

#### Don’t:

* **Avoid Redundant Badges:** Don’t add an image badge to the main product image if it already displays the same HTML badge.
* **Refrain from Overloading Promotions:** Don’t show the same badge in carousels or pages filtered by promotion type (e.g., clearance badges on the Clearance PLP should be avoided).
* **Minimize Badge Copy Length:** Badge copy should be concise due to limited space.
* **Stick to Standard Designs:** Don’t create new badge layouts or designs to maintain consistency.

By following these guidelines, QVC can ensure that badges and labels effectively highlight product differentiators, enhance user experience, and improve the overall shopping experience.

| **Do:** | **Don’t:** Add an Image Badge to 001 |
| --- | --- |
| image-20240918-142247.png | image-20240918-142139.png |

| **Do:** Group similar products for promotion and label the content with the offer title. | **Don’t:** Display Badges that repeat on every product |
| --- | --- |
| image-20240913-191033.png | image-20240913-190950.png |

---

### Visual Hierarchy

#### Today's Special Value (TSV) Badges

**The TSV is the ONLY Badge that can have the solid red background.**

* **Design:** The TSV badge features a solid red background to make it stand out from all other badges.
* **Placement:** Prominently displayed to signify the best deal of the day.
* **Purpose:** Captures immediate attention, highlighting the importance of the badge.

#### Sales Badges

* **Examples:** Sale Price, Clearance Price
* **Design:** Red text on a White background.
* **Placement:** Less prominent than the TSV badge due to their frequency and volume, designed to prevent visual clutter while still guiding customers to deals.

#### General Promotion Badges

* **Examples:** Big Deal, Beauty IQ Steal
* **Design:** Midnight blue text on a white background.
* **Purpose:** Ensures clarity and consistency for less critical promotions, maintaining a cohesive look and feel without cluttering the visual hierarchy.

#### Major Events

* **Examples:** Black Friday, Cyber Monday
* **Design:** Custom designs may be created for major events like Black Friday or Cyber Monday, but **must be discussed** with the UX Product Design team first to ensure consistency with the QVC Design System.
* **Purpose:** Ensures clarity and consistency, making it easy for users to differentiate between various promotions.

---

### How Badges are Displayed

* **Carousels and PLP Cards:** Badges are displayed directly below the image and above the pricing information (when available). Note: PLP cards are part of an upcoming update (*Coming Soon*).
* **Product Detail Pages (PDP):** On PDPs, badges appear at the top of the price block, maintaining the same visual order as in other locations.

![Frame 6.png](https://qurate.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/478150657/Frame%206.png?version=1&modificationDate=1726668573727&cacheVersion=1&api=v2)

---

**PDP Badge Interaction**

**Only on the Product Display Page**  
If a customer clicks or taps the Badge and popup should display a short explanation of the Promotion.

![Screenshot 2024-09-12 at 2.24.56PM.png](https://qurate.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/478150657/Screenshot%202024-09-12%20at%202.24.56%E2%80%AFPM.png)

---

### Research

**PXP Deal Labels Max Diff Study**

Objective: **to get customer feedback on which label they think offers the most value or is the most important to them when making a purchase.**

* **Free Standard S&H** and **Today’s Special Value** were left out of the final list of deal labels tested. This is due to the overwhelming preference found in the first round of testing that included all 30 deal labels. **Customers found these two terms 8x+ more appealing than any other deal label presented.** This is not surprising as customers were most aware of these two terms on an unaided and aided basis. 
* The original study intended to test 30 deal labels. Preliminary results suggested that testing 28 deal labels would yield more actionable results. 10 of the 28 labels were found to be more appealing than the others tested.
* **One Time Only Price** was found to be 5x+ more appealing than any other deal label presented. 
* If deciding to display two deal labels at the same time, One Time Only Price and Today Only Price would appeal to the most customers (36.7%)
* **One Time Only Price** on its own reaches 21.4% of customers.
* The addition of Today Only Price would bump that reach up to 36.7%\*

![Screenshot 2024-09-16 at 2.50.04PM.png](https://qurate.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/478150657/Screenshot%202024-09-16%20at%202.50.04%E2%80%AFPM.png)

### New Badge Creation Process

* **Creating New Badges:** If a specific badge or pricing code is not listed, create one using the UX Design Guidelines for Badge Visual Hierarchy.
* **Consultation:** Contact the UX Product Design team for any questions or clarifications before implementation to ensure alignment with product standards and compliance.

**Sources:**

* Baymard Institute Consider Using Labels and Badges to Selectively Highlight Unique Purchasing Attributes in List Items #447: [Link](https://baymard.com/premium/guideline-collections/vnxizq/447?summaryPermalink=vnxizq&guidelineCitationNumber=447)
* PXP Deal Labels Max Diff Study: [Link](https://qvc.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/GRP-QurateResearchLibrary/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B2EF9FE86-EE42-452A-BB4B-D48F23BA21B6%7D&file=PXP%20Deal%20Labels%20Report%20-%20January%202023.pptx&action=edit&mobileredirect=true)
* W3 Accessibility Guidelines: [Link](https://www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/wcag/)
* Tableau TPS Product Information: System you can look for products that are associated with LIVE Price Codes: [Link](https://tableau-colo.qrg.one/#/views/TPSProductInformation/PriceReasonCodes?:iid=1)
